# Scan Azure DevOps for secrets

Connect Azure DevOps as a data source to HCP Vault Radar to scan your Azure DevOps projects
for sensitive data and secrets. Vault Radar supports scanning Azure DevOps
Server and Azure DevOps Cloud.

@include 'vault-radar/radar-quickstart-tutorials.mdx'

## Prerequisites

- HCP IAM user with the HCP owner, or admin role
- [Vault Radar agent](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) deployed and running (agent scan only)
- Access to an Azure DevOps organization and project
- A [personal access token](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/organizations/accounts/use-personal-access-tokens-to-authenticate?view=azure-devops&tabs=Windows)
  with access scoped to **All accessible organizations** with the following
  permission scopes:

  ```plaintext
  Code > Read
  Entitlements > Read
  Graph > Read
  Identity > Read
  Member Entitlement Management > Read
  Notifications > Read, Write, Manage
  Project and Team > Read
  Service Connections > Read
  User Profile > Read
  Work Items > Read, Write
  ```

## Add an Azure DevOps data source

You can add and scan Azure DevOps Cloud or Azure DevOps Server using the **HCP
Vault Radar cloud scan** or **HCP Vault Radar agent scan**.

<Note title="Scan up to 5000 repositories">

Vault Radar allows you to scan up to 5000 repositories per connection. If
you have more than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000
repositories with the most recent activity.

</Note>

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Azure DevOps Cloud">

Add Azure DevOps Cloud as a data source to scan your Azure DevOps projects for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **Azure DevOps Cloud**.

1. Enter the personal access token in the **Enter your Azure DevOps personal
   access token** field.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **Azure DevOps Cloud**.

1. Enter the path to the environment variable with the personal access token in
   the **Enter environment path to your Azure DevOps personal access token**
   field.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Azure DevOps Server">

Add Azure DevOps Server as a data source to scan your Azure DevOps projects for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **Azure DevOps Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your Azure DevOps domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your Azure DevOps Server.
   - **Enter your Azure DevOps token:** Access token from your Azure DevOps
     Server.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **Azure DevOps Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your Azure DevOps domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your Azure DevOps Server.
   - **Enter environment path to your Azure DevOps token:** Access token from your Azure DevOps
     Server.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Assign a group to a resource

@include 'vault-radar/add-group-to-resource.mdx'

## Update data source host

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Source Hosts**, and click the three dots to the right.

1. To update the monitored data sources, click **Edit data sources**

1. To update the token, click **Edit data source host details**
